title: Pattern Candidates To Add
description: Pattern candidates surfaced during composition research but not yet catalogued. Each entry names what the pattern would forbid or enable, the composition that raised it, and the upstream evidence. Entries graduate by being authored into patterns-src/ and removed from this list.

candidates.name: Managed Agent Runtime
candidates.summary: Offer the agent loop itself as a managed cloud primitive: the caller supplies a model, system prompt and tools, and the platform runs the orchestration loop in an isolated, session-scoped sandbox with built-in memory, identity and observability — rather than each team hand-rolling and operating its own loop.

raised_by.note: AgentCore Harness + Runtime expose the agent loop as a managed service with microVM session isolation. Distinct from react (the loop shape) and sandbox-isolation (one mechanism) — the pattern is the loop-as-a-service contract. Corroborated by serverless agent runtimes generally (e.g. Cloudflare/Modal-style execution).

evidence.title: What is Amazon Bedrock AgentCore? (Harness)
evidence.quote: A managed agent loop that lets you define and invoke AI agents with a single API call... Each session runs in an isolated microVM with filesystem and shell access

candidates.name: Local-to-Cloud Handoff
candidates.summary: Let an interactive local agent session be promoted mid-task to a cloud agent that keeps running after the developer disconnects, then report back asynchronously — decoupling the human's presence from the agent's progress.

raised_by.note: Jules runs the approved plan autonomously in a cloud VM and notifies on completion. Distinct from handoff (agent-to-agent control) and agent-resumption (resuming a saved session) — the force is detaching execution from the local interactive session. Cross-product: Cursor CLI 'Cloud Handoff', Claude Code cloud routines, Antigravity.

evidence.title: Jules docs
evidence.quote: autonomously — so you can move on while it handles the task

raised_by.note: Cursor CLI 'Cloud Handoff' reportedly pushes the current conversation to a cloud agent that keeps running while the laptop is closed. Needs a primary-source quote (surfaced via CLI-agent roundup, not yet fetched from Cursor docs).

evidence.title: The 2026 Guide to Coding CLI Tools (Tembo)

candidates.name: Full-Desktop Computer Use
candidates.summary: Give the agent a complete containerized OS desktop — native apps, persistent filesystem, password-manager/2FA auth — rather than a browser-only or single-app surface, so it can complete multi-application workflows a web-only agent cannot.

raised_by.note: MAY BE A VARIANT of computer-use + sandbox-isolation rather than a distinct pattern — flag for the authoring pass to decide. The distinguishing force is full-OS scope (native apps + persistent FS + desktop credential stores) vs browser/GUI-single-app. Single source; needs corroboration.

evidence.title: bytebot-ai/bytebot
evidence.quote: When AI has access to a complete desktop environment, it unlocks capabilities that aren't possible with browser-only agents or API integrations.

candidates.name: Agent Credential Vault
candidates.summary: Broker the agent's credentials at action time through a managed vault of passwords, MFA secrets and digital personas, so secrets are never placed in the prompt or context and the agent authenticates as a governed identity.

raised_by.note: Notte bundles a credential vault (passwords, MFA, digital personas) the web agent draws on automatically. MAY OVERLAP with secrets-handling and delegated-agent-authorization — flag whether the runtime-vault-with-personas shape is distinct enough. Corroborated by AgentCore Identity.

evidence.title: nottelabs/notte
evidence.quote: Best framework to build web agents, and deploy serverless web automation functions on reliable browser infra.

raised_by.note: AgentCore Identity provides agent identity/access management and credential providers compatible with existing IdPs (Cognito, Okta, Entra ID, Auth0).
